verdict.label: Probably skip
verdict.headline: No reliable window
verdict.reason: Surfline and Surf-Forecast agree that the useful windows are poor.

conversationalSummary.text: TODAY is probably one to skip—there’s no reliable window, and Rey Cortiço at lunch is just the least-bad call. Wind looks off and Surfline consistency is shaky, so even if there’s a tiny bit of sandbank life, it could easily turn heavy/messy. I’d check Surfline and the MEO Beachcam before leaving and only head out if the cams look surprisingly clean; otherwise, save the drive.

best.windFit: NW 15 km/h · onshore · poor wind fit
best.bestWind: ideal E/ENE/ESE, usable NE/SE, clean <=22 km/h

photo.alt: Photo of Rey Cortiço surf spot

wetsuit.reason: 4/3 remains the safer default for Peniche unless water is warm and wind is light.

best.explanation: Rey Cortiço looks like a small window for Rare window, Low confidence: 0-0.3 m, 16 kJ, 10 s period, lully 1/100, and wind is the main risk.  An open beach break where the sandbanks decide everything; this is best for quiet, punchy peaks when Lagide is too small; 10-13s at low tide can still work well on Belgas.  Watch: Surfline consistency is lully; wind angle is poor for this spot; exposed north spot probably too small.
best.shortExplanation: Rey Cortiço is worth a quick check for lunch, but it is not a guaranteed session.  Treat it as a check-first call rather than a sure thing.  Wind is the main thing to watch.  The tide should let the sandbars work without overdoing it.
warnings: wind angle is poor for this spot
exposed north spot probably too small
